epic.arc: The foreign review of exports/qa-metabolism/ comes home as work. Each finding is a proposed atom under this one arc; bdo's confirm-arc queues them all, his decline retires them — either way the review stops stranding as context.
epic.context: Returned through the inbound envoy seam (done-line 0057) against seal 8538e3ef1cd0… on exports/log.jsonl. The reviewer saw a sealed snapshot, so every finding is PROPOSED and unverified: the value gate judges each story and a session verifies its claim against the live repo before any is acted on.
epic.value: A foreign review becomes value-gated, queued work instead of read-only context — the return leg the one-way envoy lacked.
epic.horizon: Every finding judged by the value gate and either carried under bdo's confirmed arc or refused; none hand-carried into a doc.

pieces.glue: The refuse-token count (370) featured in the sealed package is a vanity metric: it counts a proxy, not the property, the way coverage% does. Assertion density without per-assertion messages is Assertion Roulette. Retire the count; keep asserted, message-bearing negative-path coverage instead.

The anti-constant grep kills one mutant in one file; that is not a mutation score. Either run a real mutation lane and report an actual score, or keep the grep as a labelled smoke-check and stop calling it oracle-gap coverage. No third framing.

Pin every QA handle to its standard-practice name (log-is-truth to event-sourcing/CQRS, atom=sha256 to content-addressed storage, section-10 to negative-path testing) so the repo inherits solved failure-modes instead of rediscovering them in retros.

The non-overridable enforcement (atomless PR refuses, mock-only receipt refuses, enforced in the merge fold not a settings toggle) exceeds industry baseline, where quality gates ship with admin override. Name it in standard terms and protect it.
Mock-vs-real as an auditable runtime admission that screams every turn until resolved (mock-shame) is above the baseline naming-convention hygiene for test doubles. Protect it.

Build a repeatable test-QA pipeline as a skill: a definition-of-done for tests, a type-dispatched review rubric, a non-bypassable gate, and a self-surfacing backlog, composing standard components (linter, LLM-review, mutation lane, metrics report). Its value is the composition and enforcement, not invented parts.
